questionText: What is the maximum permitted length for a mobile home power-supply cord?
options: 21 feet
25 feet
30 feet
36½ feet
explanation: Per NEC 550.10(D), the power-supply cord must not exceed 36½ feet (11.0 m) in length, measured from the attachment plug cap to the point of entrance to the mobile home.
sparkyTip: Max cord = 36½ feet, min = 21 feet. The cord needs to reach the pedestal but not be so long it creates a hazard.

questionText: In a mobile home, the grounded (neutral) conductor and the equipment grounding conductor must be:
options: Bonded together at the mobile home panelboard
Connected at the power-supply cord plug
Kept separate throughout the mobile home
Bonded at any accessible point in the home
explanation: Per NEC 550.16(A), the grounded (neutral) conductor and the equipment grounding conductor must be kept separate in a mobile home. The neutral-to-ground bond is made at the service disconnecting means (the outdoor pedestal), not inside the mobile home.
sparkyTip: A mobile home panelboard works like a sub-panel — neutral and ground stay separate! The bond happens at the outdoor disconnect.

questionText: Where must the mobile home service equipment be located?
options: Inside the mobile home, adjacent to the panelboard
Underneath the mobile home chassis
In a readily accessible outdoor location within sight of the home
At the property line of the mobile home lot
explanation: Per NEC 550.32(A), mobile home service equipment must not be mounted in or on the mobile home. It must be located in a readily accessible outdoor location and within sight from the mobile home it serves.
sparkyTip: Service equipment goes OUTSIDE and within sight — think of the familiar power pedestal standing next to the home. Never inside or under the unit!

questionText: What is the minimum permitted length for a mobile home power-supply cord?
options: 10 feet
15 feet
21 feet
25 feet
explanation: Per NEC 550.10(D), the power-supply cord must be not less than 21 feet (6.4 m) in length, measured from the attachment plug cap to the point of entrance to the mobile home.
sparkyTip: Min 21 feet, max 36½ feet. The minimum ensures you can reach the disconnect pedestal from most lot configurations.

questionText: The mobile home power-supply cord must be permanently connected to which component?
options: An exterior-mounted junction box on the chassis
A receptacle outlet on the mobile home frame
The distribution panelboard or a junction box inside the mobile home
The main service disconnect at the lot pedestal
explanation: Per NEC 550.10(B), the power-supply cord must be permanently connected to the distribution panelboard or to a junction box permanently connected to the panelboard, with both located inside the mobile home.
sparkyTip: The cord connects inside to the panel — it is a permanent connection, not a plug-in. The only plug is at the other end, at the lot pedestal.
